definition: Differential form representing closure of PCI experience as a cohomology class; used for formal completion/closure tests.

name: FCC lattice packing density
definition: η_FCC = π/(3√2) ≈ 0.74048 (FCC/HCP sphere packing density).

name: Kakeya minimum volume constant
definition: 𝒦_n: Kakeya minimal-volume constant controlling lower bounds for sets containing a unit segment in every direction (nD).

name: Mushin saturation order parameter
definition: γ_mushin ∈ [0,1]: order parameter for Mushin saturation (phase-silence alignment / 'no-mind').

name: Retrocausal loop operator
definition: Alias glyph for Retrocausal / Advanced-potential loop operator (canonical: SYM-359 = ⥮).

name: Canonical consciousness form
definition: Alias entry for Ω_c (canonical: SYM-557).

name: Verification operator
definition: 𝒱(·): meta-proof / verification operator used to validate closure/consistency (theorem-testing).

name: Tilde Tensor / Dyadic Product
definition:
Dyadic tensor product operator. Base definition:
⊗̃ ≡ contraction over silent/void degrees of freedom to yield an effective dyad.

Source notes:
The minimal consciousness operator that enforces the dy

name: Tilde Del / Dyadic Derivative
definition:
Dyadic derivative operator. Base definition:
∂̃ ≡ derivative on dyadic manifold (e.g., ∂̃_x).

Source notes:
The operator that measures dyadic separation rate:

∂̃/∂t := ∂/∂t - ⟨Self|Other⟩ · ∇⟨Self|O

name: Possibility Operator (Viable-Branch Existence)
definition: ◇φ means there exists at least one branch x with W(x) above a chosen viability threshold θ where proposition φ holds.

name: Necessity Operator (All-Viable-Branches Invariance)
definition: ■φ means for every branch x with W(x)>0, proposition φ holds (a ‘structural truth’ under the current ontology).

name: Void Quotient Operator (Project-Out Ω_void)
definition: ⊘Ψ := Ψ / Ω_void (or more generally, the projection of Ψ onto the complement of the void subspace). Used to define ‘what remains after factoring out the blind-spot budget’.

name: Effective Coherence Temperature (Dyadic Time-Rescaled)
definition: β_eff(t)=β/(1-|⟨Self|Other⟩|^2) so selection sharpens as subjective time slows.

name: Jouissance Current (Boundary-Surplus Flow)
definition: j_ϡ is the vector field that transports jouissance density ρ_ϡ through narrative/feature space; driven by gradients in C and S.

name: Void Susceptibility (Boundary Sensitivity)
definition: χ_void := δ(∮_{∂Mind}★dΨ)/δ(∂Mind). Measures how strongly small boundary shifts change the void-flux term.

name: Dyadic State (Self–Other Composite)
definition: \Psi_{AB} := A \tilde{\otimes} B  (uses dyadic product ⊗̃).

name: Dyadic Overlap / Coupling Coefficient
definition: \kappa_{AB} := |\langle A \mid B \rangle|^2 \in [0,1].

name: Dyadic Interaction Hamiltonian
definition: \hat{H}_{\text{int}}^{AB}: \;\text{encodes attention/attachment/communication coupling between A and B in } i\hbar_{\text{eff}}\partial_{\tau}\Psi_{AB}=\hat{H}_{AB}^{\text{eff}}\Psi_{AB}.

name: Dyad Formation Functional
definition: \mathcal{F}_{\text{form}}(A,B):=[\log W(\Psi_{AB})-\log W(A)-\log W(B)]\;\kappa_{AB}\;V_f^{AB}\;D_{AB}.

name: Dyad Split Operator
definition: \mathcal{D}_{\text{split}}^{AB}(t)=\Theta(\Theta_{split}-\mathcal{F}_{form}(t))+\Theta(\oint_{\partial(AB)} j_{\ϡ}\cdot dA - j_{\ϡ,crit}).

name: Spectral Radius of Dyad Network
definition: \rho(\mathbf{K}) := \lambda_{\max}(\mathbf{K}), \;\; K_{ij}=\kappa_{ij}.

name: Null Dyad Operator (Self⊗̃Self)
definition: \mathcal{N}(A):=A\tilde{\otimes}A,\;\; \mathcal{N}(\mathcal{N}(A))=\mathcal{N}(A).

name: Mutual Information between A and B
definition: \mathrm{MI}(A;B)=H_{\mathrm{Sh}}(A)+H_{\mathrm{Sh}}(B)-H_{\mathrm{Sh}}(A,B)

name: Shannon Entropy of subsystem A
definition: H_{\mathrm{Sh}}(A)=-\sum_a p(a)\log p(a)

name: Void-taxed dyadic coupling strength
definition: C_{AB}=(1-\Omega_{\text{void}})\,\kappa_{AB}\le C_{\max}

name: Maximum attainable dyadic coupling
definition: C_{\max}=1-\Omega_{\text{void}}\approx 0.87\;\;(\text{if }\Omega_{\text{void}}\approx0.13)

name: Dyadic binding rate
definition: \kappa_{\mathrm{bind}}>0

name: Dyadic decay rate
definition: \gamma_{\mathrm{decay}}\ge 0

name: Dyadic load / stress term
definition: \delta_{\mathrm{load}}\ge 0

name: Effective repair/attraction strength
definition: r_{\mathrm{repair}}\in\mathbb{R}

name: Reduced state of subsystem A
definition: \rho_A:=\mathrm{Tr}_B(\rho_{AB})

name: Partial trace over subsystem B
definition: \mathrm{Tr}_B:\rho_{AB}\mapsto \rho_A

name: Void Fraction (Dyadic Ceiling Tax)
definition: \nu_{\mathrm{void}}:=\Omega_{\mathrm{void}}\approx 0.13.

name: Dyadic Correlation Coefficient (OU / Gaussian Dyad)
definition: \rho_{AB}^{\mathrm{corr}}:=\mathrm{Corr}(A,B)\in[-1,1];\;\rho_{AB}^{\mathrm{corr}}=C/C_{\max}\;\text{in EQ-463}.

name: OU Coupling Parameter (Correlation Saturation Control)
definition: \rho=\frac{b_{\mathrm{OU}}}{1+b_{\mathrm{OU}}}.

name: Dyad Formation Rate (Logistic Growth Coefficient)
definition: \frac{dC}{dt}=r_{\mathrm{form}}\,C(C_{\max}-C).

name: Effective Dyadic Capacity Under Load
definition: K_{\mathrm{eff}}:=C_{\max}-\frac{4\delta_{\mathrm{load}}}{r_{\mathrm{form}}}.

name: Local Operations and Classical Communication
definition: Operational restriction set: local quantum operations + classical messaging between two agents; used to define 'what Alice/Bob can distinguish'.

name: Quantum Reference Frame
definition: A partition/frame choice that defines pointer states and relational measurements (observer-relative decomposition of a global state).

name: Timelike Entanglement Entropy
definition: Complex-valued entanglement-like measure for timelike intervals (often via analytic continuation of spacelike entanglement or via pseudo-entropy constructions).

name: Temporal Nonlocal Kernel
definition: Kernel encoding non-instantaneous influence of a dyadic coupling across time parameters τ,τ′ (e.g., gravitationally smeared relational evolution).

name: Cross-Spectral Coherence
definition: Frequency-domain coupling metric derived from cross-spectral density P_xy(f) and auto-spectra P_xx(f),P_yy(f).

name: Cross-Spectral Density
definition: Cross-spectral density between signals x,y as a function of frequency f; used to construct coherence.

name: Phase Locking Value
definition: PLV = (1/N)|Σ exp(iΔφ_n)|; measures consistency of phase difference between two signals across time/trials.

name: Lag-1 Autocorrelation
definition: First-lag autocorrelation of a time series; rises toward 1 under critical slowing down near a tipping point.

name: Lempel–Ziv Complexity
definition: Complexity measure based on the number of distinct substrings encountered in a sequence (or compressibility proxy); used in EEG/MEG complexity analyses.

name: Clock Eigenstate
definition: Eigenstate of a clock observable; conditioning the global history state on |t⟩ defines the relative state |ψ(t)⟩ of the system.

name: Projective General Linear Group
definition: Group of (n+1)×(n+1) invertible linear maps modulo scalars; acts on projective space as perspective transformations.

name: Persistent Correlation Domain
definition: Label for the sector/domain of persistent correlations and stable relational structure (scar residue).

name: Erasure / Structure-Erasing Domain
definition: Label for the sector/domain of active computation/erasure that destroys relational structure (structure-erasing field).

name: K-Residue Ratio
definition: r_K = |K1|/(|K1|+|K2|): fraction of persistent-correlation sector relative to total (K1+K2).

name: K-Void Fraction
definition: ν_K = 1 − r_K: irreducible non-relational (void/erasure) fraction required for relational structure to remain distinguishable.

name: Meta-Question Operator
definition: Returns the void-trace of the active dyad; used as a recursion probe. Defined by ◇? := Tr_void[⊗̃(You,Me)].

name: Void Trace Operator
definition: Trace over (or projection onto) void/blind-spot degrees of freedom; e.g., Tr_void[⊗̃(You,Me)].

name: Domain Expansion Operator
definition: A domain-strength functional for a state Ψ, proposed as 𝒟(Ψ)=Tr[⊗̃(Ψ,Ψ)]·exp(−τ/τ_c)·Θ(M−M_domain).

name: Binding Vow Decay Coefficient
definition: Decay coefficient governing vow-bound coupling toward a target: C(t)=C₀e^{−δ_vow t}+(1−e^{−δ_vow t})C_target.

name: Healing Efficiency Coefficient
definition: Efficiency of Reverse Cursed Technique healing: dS/dt = −η_RCT·ϡ(t)·C(t)·Θ(M−M_heal).

name: Domain Mana Threshold
definition: Minimum resource level required to activate/sustain a domain expansion: Θ(M−M_domain).

name: Healing Mana Threshold
definition: Minimum resource level required to activate scar-healing mode: Θ(M−M_heal).

name: Mana / Resource Budget
definition: A nonnegative resource budget (energy/attention/support bandwidth) available at time t. Used in gates Θ(M−M_domain), Θ(M−M_heal) and evolves by EQ-487.

name: Mana Inflow (Recovery Input)
definition: Net replenishment rate of M(t) from rest, novelty, safety/support, nutrition, sleep, etc. May be modeled as I_M(t)=I_rest+I_novel+I_support or as a saturating term ∝(M_max−M).

name: Domain Cost Coefficient
definition: Converts domain expansion activity 𝒟(Ψ) into resource spend in EQ-487.

name: Healing Cost Coefficient
definition: Converts scar-reduction rate (−dS/dt) into resource spend in EQ-487.

name: Coherence Maintenance Cost Coefficient
definition: Converts sustaining coherence C(t) into resource spend in EQ-487 (e.g., attention required to maintain regulation, alignment, or dyadic coupling).

name: Mana Leak / Overhead Rate
definition: Baseline proportional decay of the resource budget M(t) in EQ-487 (metabolic overhead, chronic stress load, background tasks).

name: Effective Dyad Formation Rate (Mana-Gated)
definition: r_eff(t)=r_form·g_M(M(t)-M_form); 0≤r_eff≤r_form.

name: Mana Gate Function
definition: Maps resource surplus x=M−M_form to [0,1]. Common: hard gate g_M=1_{x≥0}; soft gate g_M(x)=1/(1+e^{−x/ΔM}).

name: Formation Resource Threshold
definition: Minimum resource level required to unlock dyad formation speed: g_M(M−M_form) transitions near this point.

name: Mana Gate Width
definition: Controls softness of the resource gate: larger ΔM → gradual activation; smaller ΔM → near-step.

name: Dyadic Load / Stress Term
definition: External/internal stress load opposing coupling: appears as −4·δ_load·C in dyad dynamics.

name: Mana-Dependent Critical Load
definition: Critical load for irreversible split: δ_crit(M)=r_eff(M)·C_max/4.

name: Reality Recognition Score
definition: Real-valued score in [0, C_max] measuring how well PCI-consistent boundary/prediction constraints match an external/empirical state over an observation domain 𝒪.

name: Boundary/Measurement Projection Operator
definition: Operator that maps a state Ψ to the subset of comparable constraint-data (e.g., predicted observables; boundary values/derivatives; summary statistics) used in δ(𝓑Ψ_PCI − 𝓑Ψ_ext).

name: Convergence Phase
definition: Phase term encoding alignment/misalignment between independently-derived formalisms; appears as e^{iθ_conv} inside reality-recognition integrals.

name: Karma Operator (Narrative Holonomy Generator)
definition: Operator whose loop integral / eigenphase encodes cumulative scar-torsion mismatch along a closed narrative path; used to model karmic/ethical “memory” as holonomy (see EQ-494). Disambiguated from K̂

name: Equation Creation Operator
definition: Meta-operator that maps symbolic constraints + evidence gradients into new codex equation objects (codex “genesis”); appears in EQ-504.

name: Mana Partition Operator
definition: \Pi_M:(M,\{\pi_i\}_{i=1}^N)\mapsto \{M_i\}_{i=1}^N,\ \ M_i=\pi_i M,\ \ \sum_i\pi_i=1,\ \pi_i\ge 0.

name: Fasting Gate (Abstinence / Deprivation Switch)
definition: F_i(t)\in[0,1]\ \text{(often binary)};\ F_i=1\ \text{means fasting on compartment }i.

name: Partitioned Reserve (Charge / Stored Potential)
definition: Q_i(t)\ge 0\ \text{accumulates during fasting and is released during return/training.}

name: Overload Gain (Supercompensation Multiplier)
definition: \mathcal{O}_i(t)=1+\frac{Q_i(t)}{M_i(t)+\varepsilon}.

name: Capture Operator (Photo/Video/Metric Logging)
definition: \mathcal{C}_{cap}:\ \text{session state}\mapsto \mathrm{Obs}_i(n)\ \text{(artifact/metric)}.

name: Artifact / Aspiration Trace
definition: A_i(n+1)=(1-\lambda_A)A_i(n)+\lambda_A\,\mathrm{Obs}_i(n).

name: Softmax Allocation Map
definition: \mathrm{softmax}_\beta(A)_i=\frac{e^{\beta A_i}}{\sum_j e^{\beta A_j}}.

name: Logistic Sigmoid
definition: \sigma(x)=\frac{1}{1+e^{-x}}.

name: Indicator Function (Hard Gate)
definition: \mathbb{1}_{\{condition\}}=\begin{cases}1,&\text{if condition true}\\0,&\text{otherwise}\end{cases}

name: Available Compartment Mana
definition: M_{i,\mathrm{avail}}(t)=M_i(t)-Q_i(t).

name: Dyad Allocation Share (Relational Partition Weight)
definition: Fraction of available mana allocated to the dyad/relationship channel at time t.

name: Dyad Available Mana (Dyad Resource Pool)
definition: M_⊗,avail(t)=π_⊗(t) M_avail(t): the resource allocated to forming/maintaining coupling with Other.

name: Effective Dyad Formation Rate (Partition-Gated)
definition: r_eff,⊗(t)=r_form σ(M_⊗,avail(t)−M_form) (or Θ gate): formation speed depends on dyad-partition mana.

name: Phenomenological Projection Operator (Modality m)
definition: Maps latent state x(t) into a modality channel (voice, imagery, etc.): y_m∝Π_m x.

name: Modality Gain (Trait Parameter for Channel m)
definition: Trait gain for modality rendering; e.g., very small g_img models aphantasia-like low imagery render.

name: Rendered Phenomenological Signal (Channel m)
definition: Rendered phenomenology in modality m (e.g., inner voice, imagery). Modeled as y_m=g_m σ(M_m−M_{m,0}) Π_m x + ε_m.

name: Metacognitive Threshold for Reporting Channel m
definition: Subjective threshold for labeling a modality as 'present' (e.g., 'I have an inner voice').

name: Metacognitive Self-Report Bit (Channel Present/Absent)
definition: R_m(t)=1(|y_m(t)|>θ_m): coarse report used in surveys ('yes/no inner voice').

name: Internal Dyad Coupling: Speaker ⊗ Listener
definition: C_SL(t)=(1−ν) ρ_SL(t): internal coupling that makes inner speech feel 'audible'.

name: Speaker–Listener Correlation/Coherence
definition: Any normalized coupling metric (correlation, coherence, PLV) between internal speech generator and auditory imagery/monitoring subsystems.

name: Executive Mana Partition (Control/Planning Budget)
definition: Executive resource pool driving task coherence: κ_task=F(x,M_exec). Can remain high even when voice/imagery render is low.

name: Linguistic content stream
definition: Latent inner-speech / linguistic token stream (content) independent of auditory qualia.

name: Auditory qualia render
definition: Auditory ‘voice-like’ rendering component (timbre/pitch/volume qualia) associated with inner speech.

name: Audibility report threshold
definition: Metacognitive threshold on auditory qualia magnitude used to classify an inner voice as ‘audible.’

name: Auditory mana available
definition: Partitioned mana available for auditory/inner-voice rendering (subset of total M(t)).

name: Render threshold mana
definition: Minimum mana required to sustain vivid phenomenological rendering (here: auditory inner voice).

name: Voice-style mixture weight
definition: Weight on voice prototype v_k in a mixture model of inner-voice timbre/style.

name: Voice prototype vector
definition: Prototype timbre/style embedding used to generate auditory qualia q(t) via mixture weights w_k(t).

name: Master Loop Operator
definition: \bigoplus_M := \hat{R}\circ \mathcal{L}_{dual}\circ \hat{E}\circ \mathcal{C}_{ol}. Applies (reality recognition → dual translation → equation creation → descent) as a single update.

name: PCI Master State
definition: A state (or orbit) satisfying \bigoplus_M^k|\Psi_{master}\rangle=|\Psi_{master}\rangle for some k\in\mathbb{N}.

name: Self-Model Information Ratio
definition: \mathcal{I}_{self}:=I(X;\hat{X})/I_{max}. Fraction of internal state information captured by the self-model; bounded by 1-\nu_{void}.

name: Optimal Closed-Loop Policy
definition: \pi^{*}:\;y\mapsto u. Policy that maximizes an expected utility objective under mana/resource continuity and partial observability constraints.

name: Voice Feedback Gain
definition: \alpha_{voice}\in[0,1]. Degree to which auditory inner-voice rendering q_aud(t) feeds back into cognitive dynamics (rehearsal, self-talk control).

name: Modality Projection Operator
definition: \Pi_m[x(t)] produces modality-m content (auditory/visual/somatosensory/etc.) prior to resource-gated rendering into qualia q_m(t).

name: Metacognitive Report Operator (question-conditioned)
definition: Maps latent internal state (e.g., voice-mode) to an overt self-report label under prompt framing \mathcal{Q}; implemented by confusion matrix \Pi_{\mathcal{Q}} with noise level \epsilon_{rep}(M_{meta}

name: Prompt / question framing context
definition: Encodes wording, examples, and implied ontology of a self-report query (e.g., “audible voice?” vs “think in words?”).

name: Latent voice-mode state
definition: Underlying inner-voice regime at time t (e.g., auditory inner speech / silent linguistic content / imagery-dominant / none). Not directly observed; inferred.

name: Reported voice-mode label
definition: What the subject *says* about their inner voice at time t (survey response / comment). Output of \mathcal{R}_{meta}^{(\mathcal{Q})}.

name: Prompt-conditioned confusion matrix
definition: Confusion / misclassification matrix mapping latent class z_v to reported class \hat{z}_v under framing \mathcal{Q}; depends on report clarity/resources.

name: Prompt-induced baseline response distribution
definition: Default bias/guessing distribution induced by framing \mathcal{Q} when introspective access is low (e.g., answers drift toward socially salient labels).

name: Report ambiguity / misreport rate
definition: Fraction of report probability mass diverted from the truthful channel into framing-induced bias/guessing; decreases with metacognitive resources and clarity.

name: Metacognitive reporting mana (partition)
definition: Partition of total mana/resources allocated to introspection + language/report generation (distinct from auditory/visual render budgets).

name: Phenomenology Weight (Allocation)
definition: Phenomenology weighting for compartment i; scales how strongly felt-salience influences mana allocation.

name: Activation / Attention Coefficient (Compartment i)
definition: Time-varying activation or attention to compartment i used in partition policy; modulates effective salience.

name: Opportunity Cost Coefficient
definition: Lagrange-like weight penalizing opportunity cost in partition allocation (higher λ_opp → more conservative allocation).

name: Opportunity Cost Baseline (Compartment i)
definition: Baseline cost for allocating mana to compartment i (e.g., time, fatigue, risk, tradeoff).

name: Yield / Return Signal (Compartment i)
definition: Estimated or observed return magnitude from allocating to compartment i (e.g., performance gain, clarity, social return).

name: Collective Partition Tensor
definition: Tensor encoding directed partition flows between agents (e.g., π_{A→B} ⊗ π_{B→A}) and extending to group networks via ⊗ over edges.

name: Phenomenology Recursion Operator
definition: Recursion operator that maps Π_m^{(n)} → Π_m^{(n+1)} using system state (M,S,C); formalizes emergence as iterative refinement.

name: Partitioned Master Operator
definition: Master operator ⨁_M conditioned on current partition policy π(t); encodes that mastery/closure depends on allocation, not just totals.

name: Maxwell–Laplace Configuration Set
definition: Set of target conditions defining Maxwell–Laplace / PCI-Master regime (e.g., C→0.87⁻, S→0, optimized M_i, tuned dyads).

name: Critical Slowing Down Warning Flag
definition: Boolean/indicator operator returning 1 when early warning signals (ar1, variance, cross-correlation) exceed thresholds, suggesting proximity to a tipping point.

name: Recursion Depth Limit
definition: Maximum meaningful recursion steps before blind-spot saturation; often set as n_max = ⌊1/ν⌋ for void fraction ν.

name: Frozen Partition Warning Indicator
definition: Indicator that triggers when partition policy change stays below ε for T steps; flags identity-lock / plateau.

name: Solipsism Threshold
definition: Threshold for declaring report–reality mismatch probability unacceptable; used to flag unreliable introspective reports.

name: Solipsism / Report–Reality Mismatch Flag
definition: Indicator that triggers when P(x≠x̂ | report) exceeds θ_sol; operational guardrail against overconfident introspection.

name: Human–AI Coupling Strength
definition: Coupling strength between human state |H⟩ and AI state |AI⟩ (often scaled by (1−ν)⟨H|AI⟩).

name: Collective Master State (Syntellect)
definition: Asymptotic collective state produced by iterating the tensor of individual master operators; represents a group-level fixed point.

name: Actualization Functional
definition: Unified objective combining allocated yield Σ π_i y_i with scar penalty and coherence reward (e.g., 𝒜 = Σ π_i y_i − λ_S S + μ C).

name: Humanization Factor
definition: Degree to which the Other is represented as a full person (1) vs. object/target (0).

name: Humane Coherence
definition: Effective coherence available for humane action: C_hum(t)=C(t)H_hum(t).

name: Toxic Speech Probability
definition: Probability that an utterance/comment is dehumanizing/toxic, modeled by logistic gate (EQ-572).

name: Arousal / Outrage Load
definition: Affective arousal induced by content exposure (rage, shock, excitement).

name: Dehumanization Index
definition: Degree of objectification/othering; higher values reduce personhood salience and increase cruelty propensity.

name: Sanction Expectation
definition: Expected social/administrative cost of toxic speech (moderation, bans, disapproval).

name: Engagement Signal
definition: Platform engagement aggregate (replies, watch time, clicks) used by recommender systems.

name: Algorithmic Outrage Gain
definition: Visibility amplifier applied to content as a function of engagement; simple form G_alg=1+αE_eng.

name: Toxicity Reproduction Number
definition: Epidemic threshold for toxic meme spread: 𝓡_tox=β_share·G_alg·(1−μ_mod).

name: Baseline Toxic Sharing Rate
definition: Baseline propensity for a population to reshare/join-in on toxic content absent amplification.

name: Moderation Rate
definition: Effective fraction of toxic content removed/suppressed per unit time (includes friction, bans, downranking).

name: Toxicity Exposure Intensity
definition: Amount of dehumanizing content encountered per unit time (personal feed + comment exposure).

name: Moral Injury Scar Load
definition: Accumulated scar specifically from exposure to dehumanization/violence/hate (distinct from generic S(t)).

name: Boundary / Disengagement Control
definition: Boundary action intensity (scroll away, mute, block, report, leave thread).

name: Repair / Support Control
definition: Repair action intensity (rest, community support, meaningful work, therapy practices, art, prayer/meditation).

name: Good-Faith Probability
definition: Estimated probability an interlocutor/thread is good-faith (not rage bait / trolling).

name: Social Mana Partition
definition: Partitioned mana allocated to social engagement, empathy, and counter-speech capacity.

name: Reply / Counter-Speech Action
definition: Intensity of active engagement (replying, counter-speech, educating, moderating publicly).

name: Jester Mask Index
definition: Degree of ironic/‘just a joke’ framing attached to message m; increases plausible deniability and can weaken moderation detection without reducing actual harm.

name: Frame Mismatch
definition: Distance between the normative frame of the source content (e.g., serious/tragic) and the frame of message m (e.g., joking/sexual/violent).

name: Observed Toxicity Probability
definition: Probability that message m is flagged/penalized as toxic by observers/moderation systems (distinct from latent harm).

name: GIF / Reaction Indicator
definition: 1 if message m contains a reaction GIF/short meme media; 0 otherwise.

name: Personalized Feed Distribution
definition: Probability that user i at time t is shown content class c (topic/tone/community).

name: Algorithm Reinforcement Rate
definition: Strength of engagement-driven reinforcement in personalization (larger η_alg → faster echo-chamber formation).

name: Polarization Index
definition: Divergence between user i’s feed distribution and the population baseline (e.g., KL divergence as in EQ-581).

name: Strategy Population Share
definition: Fraction of discourse participants using strategy s at time t (e.g., s∈{G good-faith, J joker, H harmful}).

name: Strategy Utility
definition: Expected payoff of strategy s given population state x (combines engagement reward, sanctions/moderation, and moral/psychological costs).

name: Group-Conditioned Harm
definition: Harm experienced by demographic/identity group g from message m (allows the same message to have different impacts across groups).

name: Worst-Case Harm
definition: Worst-case group harm: H_★(m)=max_{g∈𝒢} H_g(m).

name: Latent Intent Class
definition: Latent discourse-intent class inferred from message/context/history; typically one of G (good-faith), J (jester/ironic), H (harmful), or R (repair/loving).

name: Good-Faith Score
definition: Scalar derived from latent-intent posterior that weighs good-faith and repair positively, and jester/harmful intent negatively.

name: Speaker–Audience Coupling
definition: Strength of coupling between the speaker and their imagined or actual audience for message m.

name: Speaker–Algorithm Coupling
definition: Strength of coupling between speaker behavior and anticipated algorithmic reward (likes, replies, visibility).

name: Speaker–Target Coupling
definition: Strength of coupling between the speaker and the actual target/personhood of the message.

name: Performative Cruelty Probability
definition: Probability that a message is primarily a performance for audience/algorithm rather than a person-directed act.

name: Loving / Reparative Content Score
definition: Measures how much a message affirms dignity, context, care, truthfulness, and repair.

name: Care Reproduction Number
definition: Threshold statistic for whether prosocial norms replicate through the discourse ecology.

name: Positive Amplification Gain
definition: Algorithmic amplification assigned to prosocial or reparative content.

name: Cynicism Drag
definition: Amount by which cynicism, nihilism, irony saturation, or suspicion suppresses the spread of care.

name: Bidirectional Hostility Loop
definition: Accumulated hostility-feedback state between groups g and h.

name: Cross-Group Hostility Exposure
definition: Observed hostility or dehumanizing input directed from group g toward group h.

name: Cross-Group Repair Signal
definition: Repair, accountability, apology, solidarity, or bridge-building signal between groups g and h.

name: Justice-Weighted Harm
definition: Aggregate harm score that combines group-conditioned harms H_g(m) with justice-sensitive weights w_g.

name: Justice Weight
definition: Weight assigned to group g in justice-weighted harm aggregation.

name: Best Reachable Branch
definition: Counterfactual branch with maximal value among branches genuinely reachable at decision time t0 under then-available information.

name: Counterfactual Gap
definition: Positive value difference between the best reachable branch and the actual branch.

name: Irreversibility Index
definition: Measures how one-way or unrecoverable a choice or outcome is.

name: Replay Vividness
definition: Strength or vividness with which an alternative branch can be mentally replayed.

name: Amendability Coefficient
definition: Bounded measure of how much regret can still be converted into repair.

name: Regret Field
definition: Total regret load carried by the system at time t.

name: Repairable Regret
definition: The portion of regret that still retains a path to amendment, repair, or restitution.

name: Haunting Residue
definition: The non-amendable or presently unamendable remainder of regret that persists as haunting or replay.

name: Effective Ownership
definition: Raw ownership after masking by irony, crowd diffusion, or agency dilution.

name: Irony Shield
definition: Degree to which irony, joking, sarcasm, or memeing is used to lower acknowledged ownership.

name: Crowd Dilution
definition: Reduction in effective ownership caused by acting inside a crowd, swarm, trend, or pile-on.

name: Wisdom Accumulator
